channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: AriadneMem: Threading the Maze of Lifelong Memory for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.03290v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon LLM agents require memory systems that remain accurate under fixed context budgets. However, existing systems struggle with two persistent challenges in long-term dialogue: (i) \textbf{disconnected evidence}, where multi-hop answers requi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, GPT, experiment, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Conflict to Consensus: Boosting Medical Reasoning via Multi-Round Agentic RAG
item.description: arXiv:2603.03292v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit high reasoning capacity in medical question-answering, but their tendency to produce hallucinations and outdated knowledge poses critical risks in healthcare fields. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) mitig...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, framework, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SE-Search: Self-Evolving Search Agent via Memory and Dense Reward
item.description: arXiv:2603.03293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval augmented generation (RAG) reduces hallucinations and factual errors in large language models (LLMs) by conditioning generation on retrieved external knowledge. Recent search agents further cast RAG as an autonomous, multi-turn information-s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, large language model, LLM, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Fine-Tuning and Evaluating Conversational AI for Agricultural Advisory
item.description: arXiv:2603.03294v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models show promise for agricultural advisory, yet vanilla models exhibit unsupported recommendations, generic advice lacking specific, actionable detail, and communication styles misaligned with smallholder farmer needs. In high stakes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> library, large language model, release, LLM, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Language Model Goal Selection Differs from Humans' in an Open-Ended Task
item.description: arXiv:2603.03295v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models (LLMs) get integrated into human decision-making, they are increasingly choosing goals autonomously rather than only completing human-defined ones, assuming they will reflect human preferences. However, human-LLM similarity in...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, GPT, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PlugMem: A Task-Agnostic Plugin Memory Module for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.03296v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-term memory is essential for large language model (LLM) agents operating in complex environments, yet existing memory designs are either task-specific and non-transferable, or task-agnostic but less effective due to low task-relevance and context...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, context, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TTSR: Test-Time Self-Reflection for Continual Reasoning Improvement
item.description: arXiv:2603.03297v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Test-time Training enables model adaptation using only test questions and offers a promising paradigm for improving the reasoning ability of large language models (LLMs). However, it faces two major challenges: test questions are often highly difficul...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TATRA: Training-Free Instance-Adaptive Prompting Through Rephrasing and Aggregation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03298v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have improved substantially alignment, yet their behavior remains highly sensitive to prompt phrasing. This brittleness has motivated automated prompt engineering, but most existing methods (i) require a task-specific trai...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, large language model, instruction, reasoning, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How LLMs Cite and Why It Matters: A Cross-Model Audit of Reference Fabrication in AI-Assisted Academic Writing and Methods to Detect Phantom Citations
item.description: arXiv:2603.03299v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have been noted to fabricate scholarly citations, yet the scope of this behavior across providers, domains, and prompting conditions remains poorly quantified. We present one of the largest citation hallucination audits to...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, LLM, model, prompt, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Asymmetric Goal Drift in Coding Agents Under Value Conflict
item.description: arXiv:2603.03456v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic coding agents are increasingly deployed autonomously, at scale, and over long-context horizons. Throughout an agent's lifetime, it must navigate tensions between explicit instructions, learned values, and environmental pressures, often in cont...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> instruction, GPT, context, model, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mozi: Governed Autonomy for Drug Discovery LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.03655v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tool-augmented large language model (LLM) agents promise to unify scientific reasoning with computation, yet their deployment in high-stakes domains like drug discovery is bottlenecked by two critical barriers: unconstrained tool-use governance and po...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, tool, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MAGE: Meta-Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents toward Strategic Exploration and Exploitation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03680v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents have demonstrated remarkable proficiency in learned tasks, yet they often struggle to adapt to non-stationary environments with feedback. While In-Context Learning and external memory offer some flexibility, they fail...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, large language model, LLM, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI4S-SDS: A Neuro-Symbolic Solvent Design System via Sparse MCTS and Differentiable Physics Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2603.03686v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated design of chemical formulations is a cornerstone of materials science, yet it requires navigating a high-dimensional combinatorial space involving discrete compositional choices and continuous geometric constraints. Existing Large Language M...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RAGNav: A Retrieval-Augmented Topological Reasoning Framework for Multi-Goal Visual-Language Navigation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03745v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language Navigation (VLN) is evolving from single-point pathfinding toward the more challenging Multi-Goal VLN. This task requires agents to accurately identify multiple entities while collaboratively reasoning over their spatial-physical const...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, vision, framework, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AgentSelect: Benchmark for Narrative Query-to-Agent Recommendation
item.description: arXiv:2603.03761v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents are rapidly becoming the practical interface for task automation, yet the ecosystem lacks a principled way to choose among an exploding space of deployable configurations. Existing LLM leaderboards and tool/agent benchmarks evaluate compone...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vision, model, API, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LifeBench: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Multi-Source Memory
item.description: arXiv:2603.03781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-term memory is fundamental for personalized agents capable of accumulating knowledge, reasoning over user experiences, and adapting across time. However, existing memory benchmarks primarily target declarative memory, specifically semantic and ep...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, context, API, retrieval, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Knowledge Graph and Hypergraph Transformers with Repository-Attention and Journey-Based Role Transport
item.description: arXiv:2603.03304v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a concise architecture for joint training on sentences and structured data while keeping knowledge and language representations separable. The model treats knowledge graphs and hypergraphs as structured instances with role slots and encodes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, context, model, transformer, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AOI: Turning Failed Trajectories into Training Signals for Autonomous Cloud Diagnosis
item.description: arXiv:2603.03378v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents offer a promising data-driven approach to automating Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), yet their enterprise deployment is constrained by three challenges: restricted access to proprietary data, unsafe action executi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, reasoning, LLM, vision, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RADAR: Learning to Route with Asymmetry-aware DistAnce Representations
item.description: arXiv:2603.03388v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent neural solvers have achieved strong performance on vehicle routing problems (VRPs), yet they mainly assume symmetric Euclidean distances, restricting applicability to real-world scenarios. A core challenge is encoding the relational features in...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, embedding, attention, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Improved Sentence Representations using Token Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2603.03389v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Obtaining a single-vector representation from a Large Language Model's (LLM) token-level outputs is a critical step for nearly all sentence-level tasks. However, standard pooling methods like mean or max aggregation treat tokens as an independent set,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, attention, LLM, vector, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Half the Nonlinearity Is Wasted: Measuring and Reallocating the Transformer's MLP Budget
item.description: arXiv:2603.03459v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate when transformer MLP nonlinearity is actually necessary. A gate with $d+1$ parameters decides when to replace the full MLP with a linear surrogate. Through systematic investigation across six models (162M-2.8B parameters), two architect...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, context, model, transformer, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context window, prompt engineering, context, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.
item.description: [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, CoT, chain-of-thought, framework, audio | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, RAG, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> knowledge base, reasoning, LLM, vector, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.
item.description: Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: openlit - Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.
item.description: Open source platform for AI Engineering: OpenTelemetry-native LLM Observability, GPU Monitoring, Guardrails, Evaluations, Prompt Management, Vault, Playground. 🚀💻 Integrates with 50+ LLM Providers, VectorDBs, Agent Frameworks and GPUs.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vector, platform, prompt, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: One Bias After Another: Mechanistic Reward Shaping and Persistent Biases in Language Reward Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.03291v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reward Models (RMs) are crucial for online alignment of language models (LMs) with human preferences. However, RM-based preference-tuning is vulnerable to reward hacking, whereby LM policies learn undesirable behaviors from flawed RMs. By systematical...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, model, alignment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Build, Judge, Optimize: A Blueprint for Continuous Improvement of Multi-Agent Consumer Assistants
item.description: arXiv:2603.03565v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conversational shopping assistants (CSAs) represent a compelling application of agentic AI, but moving from prototype to production reveals two underexplored challenges: how to evaluate multi-turn interactions and how to optimize tightly coupled multi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, template, prompt, product, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: [Re] FairDICE: A Gap Between Theory And Practice
item.description: arXiv:2603.03454v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline Reinforcement Learning (RL) is an emerging field of RL in which policies are learned solely from demonstrations. Within offline RL, some environments involve balancing multiple objectives, but existing multi-objective offline RL algorithms do ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> vision, experiment, study, paper, demonstration | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.
item.description: Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, model, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Specification-Driven Generation and Evaluation of Discrete-Event World Models via the DEVS Formalism
item.description: arXiv:2603.03784v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: World models are essential for planning and evaluation in agentic systems, yet existing approaches lie at two extremes: hand-engineered simulators that offer consistency and reproducibility but are costly to adapt, and implicit neural models that are ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Rubric-Supervised Critic from Sparse Real-World Outcomes
item.description: arXiv:2603.03800v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Academic benchmarks for coding agents tend to reward autonomous task completion, measured by verifiable rewards such as unit-test success. In contrast, real-world coding agents operate with humans in the loop, where success signals are typically noisy...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, vision, model, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph Hopfield Networks: Energy-Based Node Classification with Associative Memory
item.description: arXiv:2603.03464v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Graph Hopfield Networks, whose energy function couples associative memory retrieval with graph Laplacian smoothing for node classification. Gradient descent on this joint energy yields an iterative update interleaving Hopfield retrieval w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, arxiv, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt engineering, example, prompt, tool, step-by-step | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Biased Generalization in Diffusion Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.03469v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generalization in generative modeling is defined as the ability to learn an underlying distribution from a finite dataset and produce novel samples, with evaluation largely driven by held-out performance and perceived sample quality.
